creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
For example, from an e-mail that was received (in Thunderbird) a few hours ago:  

----

Received: from mx1.freebsd.org (mx1.freebsd.org [IPv6:2610:1c1:1:606c::19:1])
	by mlmmj.nyi.freebsd.org (Postfix) with ESMTP id 67266194F472
	for <freebsd-questions@mlmmj.nyi.freebsd.org>; Thu, 13 Jan 2022 02:40:43 +0000 (UTC)
	(envelope-from 93ab.82.c37d0000c192a6.dc718140bf7897d399f6cd5ae198b8e1@email-od.com)

----

Subject: 

> FreeBSD Trust Chain

It's now past 08:40 GMT here, the e-mail and its follow-ups are not yet at: 

<https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-questions/2022-January/date.html>

* nothing there more recent than 11th January

* I have, in Thunderbird, a variety of other e-mails that were sent 
  to the list on the 12th …
